title: Rename visible CKC module shell to Atelier and make CKC the default Atelier tab label
operator_request: The current top-right CKC button should be the Atelier button. CKC should be a tabbed window/tool inside Atelier, because CKC and PoseKit are already native Handshake Atelier tools rather than a separate top-level app.

state_recovery.recovery_rule: If a no-context model resumes this MT, first run git status in ../wtc-ckc-posekit-overhaul and ignore inherited baseline dirt unless this MT explicitly lists the file below. Do not edit ../wtc-native-editors-v1. Do not rename internal CKC identifiers.

scope.summary: Apply the smallest visible navigation correction: the top-right module button should read Atelier, while the default Atelier tab/work-surface should read CKC. The change is intentionally presentation-only.
base_scope: Change the visible top-right module button label from CKC to Atelier in the native Handshake shell.
Change the visible default Atelier tab label from Atelier to CKC where it represents the CKC character/media tool surface.
Apply the same visible label correction to the legacy React shell only if the React shell still has matching top-level module/tab label constants in the hotfix worktree.
Update focused tests that assert the old visible labels.
must_preserve: AtelierRef / CKC embed ref kinds and hsLink payloads
database, table, route, and receipt names
non_goals: Do not rebuild CKC or PoseKit behavior in this MT.
Do not change data storage, routes, API contracts, command names, receipt names, or serialized layout values.
Do not edit Facial, Leeseo, PoseKit advanced interaction, contact sheets, or ingest pipeline behavior.
Do not fix or stage unrelated inherited WP-KERNEL-012 diagnostic or Palmistry files.
acceptance_criteria: A user sees Atelier as the top-level module button where CKC used to appear.
The default tab/work-surface under that Atelier module is labeled CKC.
Existing persisted layouts that store module CKC continue to load because internal IDs are unchanged.
Stable test IDs and AccessKit identifiers used by models remain stable unless they are visible-label-only expectations.
Focused native tests are updated and pass for the new labels.
React shell labels are updated if the matching label constants still exist; React internal IDs stay unchanged.
Actual staged product diff is limited to the MT-owned label/test files plus no inherited baseline dirt.
proof_targets: Focused native module switcher label test shows the top-level visible button is Atelier and the stable id remains module-ckc.
Focused native pane header/tab test shows AtelierEditor belongs under the visible Atelier module while the default tab label reads CKC.
Search proof shows no accidental internal rename of ModuleId::Ckc, activeModule CKC, module-ckc, PaneType::AtelierEditor, or /atelier routes.

implementation_plan: 1. Update tests first so the previous CKC top-level label fails against current code.
2. Run the narrow native test target and confirm it fails for the expected visible-label mismatch.
3. Update visible label constants and nearby comments only.
4. Run rustfmt on the touched Rust files.
5. Run the focused native tests again until they pass.
6. If React label constants are touched, run the narrowest available React static/test command or record why it is not available.
7. Review git diff and stage only MT-owned files.
8. Update this MT closeout, mt_index, and packet state before final commit.

risk_notes.risk: A model may rename internal CKC identifiers instead of visible labels.
risk_notes.failure_scenario: Persisted layouts, CKC embeds, or tests that rely on module CKC break because the serialized ID changed to Atelier.
risk_notes.mitigation: The MT explicitly preserves internal CKC IDs, routes, tab keys, and data IDs. Search proof must confirm they remain.

risk_notes.risk: The label fix collides with the active GUI rebuild worktree.
risk_notes.failure_scenario: The implementation is applied to ../wtc-native-editors-v1 or stages inherited Palmistry/diagnostic work.
risk_notes.mitigation: Implementation happens only in ../wtc-ckc-posekit-overhaul. Use path-limited staging and compare against the inherited dirty baseline.

risk_notes.risk: The UI becomes semantically inconsistent: top module Atelier, but tab still Atelier.
risk_notes.failure_scenario: Operators and models still see CKC as missing or double-nested because both shell and tab use the same label.
risk_notes.mitigation: Acceptance requires visible top module Atelier and default tab CKC together.

risk_notes.failure_scenario: A no-context model opens the legacy React shell and sees the old CKC top-level label, while native says Atelier.
risk_notes.mitigation: React matching constants are in scope for label-only correction if present.

risks: Broad UI renames can silently mutate serialized IDs and break layout recovery.
A label-only MT can accidentally stage unrelated active-WP files because the source worktree was copied with inherited dirt.
Changing only native labels while leaving React labels stale may confuse later parity audits if the React shell remains a reference surface.
minimum_controls: Tests must check visible labels and stable IDs separately.
Use path-limited staging and diff review before commit.
Record any skipped React verification explicitly.

closeout.summary: MT prepared and activated. Product implementation not yet completed.
